description: Versioned snapshot of the regulatory requirements ClearMap findings are mapped against. Every finding's hipaa_ref must resolve to a citation in `regulations` below. Reports stamp this baseline_version so any assessment is traceable to the exact requirement set (and its effective dates) in force when it was produced. Bump the version when a regulation is amended or a pending change finalizes; keep prior versions for diffing.
disclaimer: Engineering target, not legal advice. ClearMap is a technical risk signal, not a HIPAA compliance certification. Counsel must confirm citations before any external claim.

interpretation_notes.addressable_is_not_optional: Per 45 CFR 164.306(d)(3), an 'Addressable' implementation specification is NOT optional. A covered entity/BA must either implement it, OR document why it is not reasonable and appropriate AND implement an equivalent alternative measure. 'Addressable' is a flexibility mechanism, not a waiver.
interpretation_notes.encryption_in_transit_is_effectively_req: Although encryption is an Addressable spec ((e)(2)(ii) in transit, (a)(2)(iv) at rest), the parent standard 164.312(e)(1) Transmission Security is Required. For ePHI over open/public networks there is no reasonable equivalent to encryption, and OCR treats unencrypted transmission over open networks as a violation. ClearMap therefore treats encryption-in-transit as REQUIRED and anchors TRANSIT findings to the Required standard 164.312(e)(1).
interpretation_notes.internal_cleartext_is_advisory: Cleartext PHI on an INTERNAL hop inside a trusted boundary (behind a VPC / load balancer, private subnet, service mesh) is a common, currently-defensible pattern: under 164.306(d)(3) an entity may document it and rely on compensating controls (network segmentation, access control). ClearMap classifies a host as internal via localhost / RFC-1918 (10., 172.16-31., 192.168.) / *.internal / *.svc.cluster.local, and reports internal cleartext PHI as a LOW-severity ADVISORY (verify trusted boundary; zero-trust best practice is TLS/mTLS internally too). EXTERNAL cleartext PHI remains a hard finding. Internal traffic with no PHI is not reported at all.

hipaa-security-technical.name: HIPAA Security Rule — Technical Safeguards
hipaa-security-technical.origin: Security Rule (2003); current text from 2013 Omnibus Rule

ocr-online-tracking.name: HHS OCR — Use of Online Tracking Technologies by HIPAA Covered Entities

onc-hti-1.name: ONC HTI-1 — Decision Support Intervention (DSI) transparency criterion

164.312(a)(2)(iv).title: Encryption and decryption
164.312(a)(2)(iv).clearmap_rationale: Addressable != optional; no reasonable alternative to encrypting ePHI at rest in most environments.

164.312(e)(2)(ii).title: Encryption (in transit)
164.312(e)(2)(ii).clearmap_rationale: Parent standard 164.312(e)(1) is Required; Addressable != optional; no reasonable equivalent to encryption for ePHI over open networks; OCR enforces.

164.306(d)(3).title: Implementation of addressable specifications (assess; implement, or document + equivalent alternative)

OCR online-tracking guidance.title: Online tracking technologies in patient-facing surfaces
OCR online-tracking guidance.scope_note: Sub-regulatory OCR guidance, not a regulation. In 2024 a federal court (American Hospital Association v. Becerra, N.D. Tex.) vacated OCR's position that tracking on UNAUTHENTICATED public webpages is regulated PHI; OCR's guidance on AUTHENTICATED, PHI-bearing surfaces stands. ClearMap flags tracking that ties health context to an identifiable patient (the durable concern); treat tracking on public marketing pages as lower risk and confirm with counsel.

ONC HTI-1 170.315(b)(11).title: Decision support intervention transparency
ONC HTI-1 170.315(b)(11).scope_note: A certification criterion for developers of ONC-certified health IT (Decision Support Interventions), not a universal HIPAA requirement for every healthcare application. ClearMap uses it as the engineering authority for clinical AI/LLM reliability and transparency; whether it legally applies to a given product depends on whether that product is, or integrates, certified health IT. Counsel confirms.

CWE-89.title: SQL Injection
CWE-89.scope_note: General application-security weakness, not a HIPAA citation. In a system holding ePHI, SQL injection can defeat access control (164.312(a)) and data integrity (164.312(c)); ClearMap reports it as a security best practice with clinical impact.

CWE-78.title: OS Command Injection
CWE-78.scope_note: General application-security weakness. Command injection yields remote code execution, defeating every technical safeguard over ePHI (164.312). Security best practice with clinical impact.

CWE-918.title: Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF)
CWE-918.scope_note: General application-security weakness. SSRF can exfiltrate ePHI or reach internal services, undermining transmission security (164.312(e)) and access control (164.312(a)).

CWE-22.title: Path Traversal
CWE-22.scope_note: General application-security weakness. Path traversal on a document store can read or overwrite arbitrary patient files, defeating access control (164.312(a)) and integrity (164.312(c)).

CWE-502.title: Deserialization of Untrusted Data
CWE-502.scope_note: General application-security weakness. Unsafe deserialization yields remote code execution over systems handling ePHI (164.312). Security best practice with clinical impact.

CWE-16.title: Security Misconfiguration (CORS / debug exposure)
CWE-16.scope_note: General application-security weakness. Wildcard CORS with credentials or a production debug endpoint can expose ePHI and defeat access control (164.312(a)); ClearMap reports it as a security best practice.

CWE-532.title: Insertion of Sensitive Information into Log File
CWE-532.scope_note: General application-security weakness. Writing full request headers (Authorization/Cookie) or raw request/response bodies to logs leaks credentials and ePHI into broadly-readable, long-lived log sinks, undermining access control (164.312(a)) and the minimum-necessary principle. ClearMap reports it as a security best practice with clinical impact.

pending_changes.title: HIPAA Security Rule To Strengthen the Cybersecurity of Electronic Protected Health Information
pending_changes.effect: Would promote encryption to a Required standalone technical-safeguard standard (at rest and in transit) and remove the Required/Addressable distinction (all implementation specs become required, with limited exceptions).
pending_changes.clearmap_stance: ClearMap already treats encryption-in-transit/at-rest as effectively required, consistent with the NPRM direction and OCR enforcement reality.
